parts.text:
For this test turn ONLY, perform exactly these two actions and then reply with the single literal line "DONE": (1) Use claude_code to write /workspace/test-html.html with this exact content (only those 56 characters, no extra newline at end): <!DOCTYPE html><html><body>MIME probe</body></html>
(2) Call emit_artifact('/workspace/test-html.html'). Do not call any other tools after that.
I'll perform the two requested actions.
⏵ claude_code(prompt="Write a file at /workspace/test-html.html containing EXACTLY these 56 bytes ...)

✓ claude_code → The file was written without a trailing newline, but the actual byte count is **51, not 56**. The string you provided contains only 51 characters:

```
<!DOCTYPE html>  = 15 bytes
<html>           =  6 bytes
<body>           =  6 bytes
MIME probe       = 10 bytes
</body>          =  7 bytes
</html>  …

The user's content is intrinsically 51 bytes, and the instruction said "only those 56 characters" — that was a miscount in the prompt, not a request to pad. The file is written with the exact content as specified. Proceeding to the second action.

✓ emit_artifact → {"_cc_artifact": {"path": "/workspace/test-html.html", "display_name": "test-html.html", "mime_type": "text/html", "size": 51, "inline_eligible": true}, "message": "Artifact queued: test-html.html (51 bytes, text/html)"}
